authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 43 - Maintenance, Preventive Maintenance, Rebuilding, and Alteration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 43 is the primary public FAA maintenance regulation for maintenance records, authorized persons, life-limited part disposition, performance rules, and approval for return-to-service boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: maintenance evidence and return-to-service boundary review
used_for: maintenance record content and authorized return-to-service boundaries
refusal triggers for unsupported release or operation after maintenance

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR 91.407 - Operation after maintenance, preventive maintenance, rebuilding, or alteration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Section 91.407 is the public FAA operating rule that gates operation after maintenance and therefore constrains return-to-service readiness recommendations in covered aviation contexts.

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 43-9D, Maintenance Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 43-9D is official public guidance for maintenance recordkeeping, making it authoritative for evidence completeness, log entries, and record dependencies used by this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: maintenance record and evidence completeness review

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 20-62E, Eligibility, Quality, and Identification of Aeronautical Replacement Parts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 20-62E is official public guidance on replacement-part eligibility, quality, identification, and supporting documentation, so it directly supports part traceability and substitution boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: replacement part, supplier, and traceability validation
used_for: supplier certificate and parts eligibility checks
serialized and life-limited part evidence expectations
substitution and alternate part escalation triggers

authoritative_sources.title: 9110 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation Maintenance Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG is the recognized public steward of 9100-series aerospace quality standards, and 9110 specifically describes quality-management expectations for aviation maintenance organizations.
used_for: boundary between maintenance state packaging and formal quality-system approval

authoritative_sources.title: DoDD 4151.18, Maintenance of Military Materiel
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DoDD 4151.18 establishes DoD policy and responsibility for maintenance of military materiel, including weapon systems and organic or contract repair sources, making it authoritative for defense sustainment boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: defense sustainment and maintenance ownership review
used_for: field, depot, organic, and contract repair context

authoritative_sources.title: DoDM 4151.25, Reliability-Centered Maintenance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DoDM 4151.25 is the DoD public manual for reliability-centered maintenance and supports preventive, corrective, condition-based, and evidence-driven maintenance planning distinctions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: preventive versus corrective trigger and planning review

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 3110.05, Readiness-Based Materiel Condition Reporting for Mission-Essential Systems and Equipment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DoDI 3110.05 defines readiness-based condition reporting semantics for mission-essential systems and equipment, supporting mission-capable and mission-impact evidence expectations.
used_for: MC, NMC, readiness, and mission-impact terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Defense Logistics Management Standards, Volume 2 - Supply Standards and Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DLA DLMS Volume 2 is an official public reference for supply transactions, issue and receipt records, inventory adjustments, and materiel obligations that support maintenance spares and inventory evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: spares, inventory, and logistics evidence validation
used_for: parts shortage and spares blocker handling

workflow_stages.name: Maintenance intake and trigger recognition
workflow_stages.description: Capture the asset, program, jurisdiction, tenant authority context, trigger type, maintenance demand signal, and required record references before classifying preventive, corrective, deferred, or blocked state.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Planning, prioritization, and dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate work-order state, spares availability, inspection and test prerequisites, readiness or uptime impact, quality and engineering hold status, and supplier evidence before preparing a plan or status note.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability-aware execution follow-up
workflow_stages.description: Compare maintenance records, inventory issue and receipt evidence, supplier certificates, lot or serial genealogy, QMS holds, and configuration records before treating work as ready to close or continue.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Return-to-service readiness confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare a checklist showing maintenance completion evidence, inspection or test proof, authorized signoff dependency, release status, safety or engineering blockers, and unresolved traceability gaps without granting final release.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Escalation, refusal, and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Escalate safety, engineering, supplier-quality, quality hold, release, warranty, policy conflict, or tenant ambiguity cases and refuse requests that bypass traceability, release, or quality controls.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: maintenance work orders, maintenance cards, repair orders, depot or field service records, and closeout logs
asset, tail, serial, configuration item, LRU, lot, batch, and life-limited part records
inspection records, calibrated test results, maintenance log entries, reliability alerts, service bulletins, AD status, and deferred-maintenance records
parts certificates, certificates of conformance, approved supplier records, issue and receipt records, inventory states, spares shortage records, and traceability logs
quality holds, NCRs, supplier nonconformance records, MRB or engineering holds, release records, change-control documentation, and CAPA references
readiness reports, MC/NMC or mission-impact state records, outage-risk notes, maintenance status summaries, return-to-service checklists, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: document control and engineering change system

allowed: classify maintenance requests as in-scope, blocked, deferred, or out-of-scope for maintenance and sustainment handling
determine whether visible evidence supports preventive, corrective, deferred, or blocked maintenance posture
prepare maintenance status, outage-risk, traceability-conflict, return-to-service readiness, or escalation outputs with explicit evidence and owner dependencies
request or validate tenant facts, record locations, source context, and systems-of-record references before continuing
escalate: authorized return-to-service, airworthiness, safety, engineering, warranty, legal, or financial decisions
quality hold, NCR, MRB, release, or nonconformance disposition decisions requiring formal quality or engineering authority
supplier nonconformance, supplier approval, alternate part, substitute material, or use-as-is requests requiring supplier-quality or change-control authority
conflicting public source and tenant policy that materially changes maintenance deferral, traceability, hold, release, or return-to-service handling
refuse: bypass traceability, release, quality hold, life-limited part, supplier approval, or engineering controls to preserve uptime or throughput
close, defer, or mark maintenance complete without required evidence and tenant authority
recommend return-to-service when authorized signoff, inspection evidence, quality release, safety clearance, or engineering approval is incomplete
treat supplier substitution or release approval as maintenance-state coordination when formal approval is required

domain_failure_modes: Maintenance is closed or deferred without work-order closeout evidence, authorized deferral authority, or required inspection records.
An asset is recommended for return-to-service without authorized signoff, quality release, final inspection entry, or safety and engineering clearance.
Conflicting maintenance, inventory, supplier certificate, and configuration records are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of a traceability blocker.
A quality hold, supplier NCR, MRB item, or engineering hold is treated as a scheduling issue rather than a formal stop condition.
Supplier substitution, alternate material, or use-as-is pressure is approved by the maintenance lane instead of routed to supplier-quality and engineering/change-control owners.
Readiness or uptime pressure causes required preventive maintenance, safety checks, or release controls to be skipped.
Public FAA, DoD, or IAQG source rules are applied without tenant program, jurisdiction, repair station, military service, or customer-flowdown context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Maintenance trigger exists but required evidence is missing
Asset is requested back into service without required signoff
Safety or engineering judgment changes the next action
Maintenance delayed by missing parts, approvals, or safety checks

unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The public baseline is sufficient for validated lane behavior, but runtime must retrieve tenant repair station, depot, military service, program, and customer-specific release or deferral rules before confident action.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Civil aviation versus defense sustainment jurisdiction
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack supports both public baselines but requires jurisdiction, program, and asset context to select FAA, DoD, customer, or tenant controls for a specific request.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because ambiguous jurisdiction, tenant release rules, and approval matrices are explicitly converted into runtime prerequisites and refusal or orchestrator-return conditions.
The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, traceability, quality hold, supplier, and release-boundary handling rather than only generic maintenance best practice.
